content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento. CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • fax (916) 322-0886 
Nicholas S. Chrisos 
County Counsel 
333 W. Santa Ana Blvd., Suite 407 
Santa Ana, CA 92701 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. W-12-065 
Dear Mr. Chrisos: 
May 8, 2012 
This letter confirms the receipt of your April 17, 2012, request for advice regarding 
CalOptima Board Member Edward Kacic, and a potential conflict of interest under the 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). The Commission can only provide formal 
written advice to someone whose duties are in question under the Act or their authorized 
representative. (Gov. Code,§ 83114, subd. (b); Cal. Code Regs., tit. 3, § 18329, subd. (b)(l).) 
In this regard, the Act requires that certain information must be provided when seeking formal 
written advice: 
"(2) Requests for formal written advice will not be acted upon unless the following 
requirements are met: 
(A) The name, title or position, and mailing address of the person whose duties 
are in question are provided. In addition, if the request is submitted by an 
authorized representative, it shall contain a specific statement that such 
authorization has been made." 
(Cal. Code Regs., tit. 3, § 18329, subd. (b)(2)(a).) 
A review of your letter reveals the absence of any statement suggesting that Mr. Kacic 
has authorized you to act as his representative in this matter. 
Moreover, it is the Commission's policy not to answer purely hypothetical questions, or 
questions that fall outside the purview of the Act. (See Cal. Code Regs., tit. 3, § 18329, subd. 
(b)(S)(D).) Your letter is generally concerned about a potential conflict of interest that may arise 
in the future if the Orange County Board of Supervisors decides to reappoint Mr. Kacic to the 
CalOptima Board. Therefore, we are withdrawing your request because it is hypothetical in 


File No. W-12-065 
Page No. 2 
nature, fails to present a question covered b.Y the Act, and does not appear to have been 
authorized by Mr. Kacic. Of course, if Mr. Kacic is reappointed and confronted with a decision 
in which he may have a conflict of interest, either he or his authorized representative can request 
advice at that time based on the specific facts surrounding that decision. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
JCW:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
By: 
odside 
Counsel, Legal Division
